summary: Ukrainian forces eliminated Russian propagandist and battalion commander Yevgeny Nikolayev ("Hayduk"), co-founder of the WarGonzo channel, in Donetsk region on March 10 via FPV drone strike, though Russian sources claim he died later in a hospital. Russian operators from the "Sever" group used "Molniya" drones to destroy Ukrainian UAV control points and pursued survivors with FPVs in Korenek, Sumy region. The Rubicon Center reported 11 targets destroyed on March 16, including Ukrainian FPVs, fixed-wing UAVs, and a Gepard self-propelled anti-aircraft gun, while also using ZALA Lancet to destroy an American M777 howitzer detected by Z-16 reconnaissance. Ukrainian forces employed novel tactics such as throwing riders off horses before FPV strikes, and Russian infantry faced unprecedented losses, including a soldier charging a drone with a bayonet and another hiding behind a tree struck by two simultaneous FPVs. Both sides advanced anti-drone systems, with Russian "Elka" drones conducting kinetic interceptions of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and Ukrainian forces countering Russian log-periodic antennas on "Molniya" drones. Iranian-backed militias also launched fiber-optic FPV attacks on U.S. Victory Base near Baghdad.
